meta.titleTemplate: Import Coconut Charcoal to Germany: Duty, VAT, EUDR
meta.descriptionTemplate: Importing UN 1361 coconut charcoal to Germany from {{port}}: 4402.20 classification, 0% duty, 19% import VAT, ICS2/ATLAS entry, and EUDR.

heroSubtitle: The German (EU) import mechanics for UN 1361 coconut charcoal from {{port}}: how it is classified, the duty and import VAT, the ICS2/ATLAS entry process, and the EUDR position on charcoal.

intro.p1: This page sits under the {{brand}} Logistics pillar and covers the customs mechanics of importing into Germany and the wider EU customs territory. The commercial case for the German market lives on the market page.

keyFacts.heading: Germany import at a glance
keyFacts.definition: Classification, headline duty, import VAT, and who clears the cargo.
keyFacts.docsValue: ICS2 ENS, ATLAS declaration, EORI, REACH proof
keyFacts.agencyValue: 4402 charcoal is in scope; the EU importer files a Due Diligence Statement

hts.body: Coconut shell charcoal falls under EU heading 4402. The correct, most specific code is 4402 20 00 00 ("of shells or nuts"); the residual 4402 90 ("other") is a documentary-mismatch risk in the automated ATLAS system. Both carry a Free base duty, so the code does not change the rate — but it must be declared identically on every document. Confirm the exact 11-digit ATLAS code with your customs broker.

duty.body: EU customs duty on heading 4402 is a single layer — 0% (Free) MFN, the same for all origins — on top of which German import VAT (Einfuhrumsatzsteuer) is charged. Read the duty as a dated layer with its legal status, and the VAT as a separate recoverable line.

landed.body: Landed cost is the sum of the goods, the freight, and the import charges. The structure below holds for any lane; the duty and VAT components are public verified figures, while the FOB and freight are illustrative and quoted at booking.
landed.fobValue: Illustrative only — your actual FOB applies
landed.note: No charcoal per-ton price is published. The formula is shown so you can plug in your own figures; we quote the live numbers at booking. Import VAT is a cash-flow event, not a permanent cost, for a VAT-registered importer.
landed.rulesLinkLabel: What your incoterm covers (FOB and the freight split)

entry.body: EU entry for a UN 1361 container follows the standard pre-arrival-then-declaration sequence, handled by the buyer's customs broker with data we supply.

dgHandling.body: The cargo arrives as declared UN 1361, Class {{unClass}} dangerous goods, and is handled accordingly at Hamburg and Bremerhaven. The classification and the prevention controls are covered on the dangerous-goods pages.

countryAgency.h2: EUDR: charcoal is in scope of the EU Deforestation Regulation
countryAgency.intro: This is regulatory information, not legal advice — see the disclaimer below.

disclaimer.heading: Regulatory information, not legal advice
disclaimer.body: This page summarizes German and EU import requirements — TARIC classification, Einfuhrumsatzsteuer, ICS2/ATLAS entry, EUDR and EU REACH — for general information only and is not legal advice. EU regulation changes without notice and enforcement is fact-specific. Confirm your obligations with a licensed German customs broker and qualified counsel before importing.

responsibilityDestLine: EUDR due diligence — filing the Due Diligence Statement (DDS) via TRACES — and EU REACH registration or Only-Representative cover for placing the charcoal on the EU market

ports.body: Indicative ocean transit from {{portWithCode}} to the main German gateways:
ports.empty: German transit times available on request.

items.q: What duty applies when importing coconut charcoal to Germany?
items.a: The EU third-country (MFN) duty on heading 4402 is 0% (Free), assessed on the customs (CIF) value. Indonesia's loss of GSP preference does not change this — MFN is already 0%. Confirm the live TARIC line and your 11-digit ATLAS code with your broker at booking.

items.q: Is German import VAT on charcoal recoverable?
items.a: German import VAT (Einfuhrumsatzsteuer) is 19%, charged on the customs value plus duty plus domestic charges. Charcoal does not qualify for the reduced 7% rate. It is fully recoverable as input tax by a VAT-registered importer with an EU/German EORI; deferment (Aufschubkonto) and input-VAT deduction apply, so it is a cash-flow event, not a permanent cost.

items.q: Does EUDR apply to coconut charcoal, who files, and when?
items.a: Yes. Heading 4402 charcoal is listed in Annex I of the EU Deforestation Regulation, with no carve-out for coconut shell. The EU importer (the first operator placing it on the market) files a Due Diligence Statement via TRACES, showing plot geolocation, deforestation-free status and legality. Application is 30 December 2026 for large/medium operators and 30 June 2027 for micro/small. We are working toward supplying the geolocation and legality evidence your DDS needs. EU REACH registration or Only-Representative cover is also required above one tonne per year — this is general information, confirm with counsel.

items.q: How long does shipping from Indonesia to Germany take?
items.a: We model the lane ex-{{portWithCode}} to the main German gateways — Hamburg and Bremerhaven — at roughly 32–45 days of sea transit; see the transit list above for the per-port range. Other German ports are available; confirm routing and the vessel schedule with the carrier at booking.
